publications.title: Towards anticipatory disaster risk financing and index insurance mechanisms for resilience building in Eastern Africa
publications.description: As climate shocks escalate in frequency and severity across Eastern Africa, the chronic reliance on post-disaster emergency aid is proving too slow, too costly, and too inadequate to protect the region's most vulnerable communities.

publications.title: Disconnected defenses: extreme weather risk across corporates, cities and financial systems
publications.description: Extreme weather is already shaping financial outcomes across the real economy.

publications.title: Assessing the resilience of global grain supplies to compound climatic and non-climatic shocks
publications.description: Using a new global bilateral trade model representing 177 countries and four major staple crops (maize, wheat, rice, soybean), the authors simulate the food supply, trade and price impacts resulting from climate-related yield variability, and shocks

publications.title: Inoperability assessment of interdependent critical infrastructures exposed to natural hazards considering climate change
publications.description: Critical Infrastructures (CIs) are interdependent and, thus, vulnerable to scenarios of cascading effects initiated by natural hazardous events such as flooding, windstorms, and heat waves, which can lead to partial or complete inoperability.

publications.title: Climate, the environment and site management – overview
publications.description: Site Management translates climate and environmental commitments into concrete, site-level action across multiple contexts.

publications.title: How much do we know about the mental health economic costs of disasters?
publications.description: This literature review focuses on the economic quantifications of the mental health consequences of disasters and how these mental health impacts can shape people’s economic trajectories.

publications.title: Under strain- international insights into electricity grid outages
publications.description: As climate change accelerates, the resilience of electricity infrastructure is emerging as a critical challenge for countries worldwide.

publications.title: Assessment of land subsidence and future flood hazards for coastal cities of the Gulf of Mexico using time series InSAR
publications.description: As sea levels rise and urban land continues to sink, coastal cities along the Gulf of Mexico face a compounding flood threat that demands urgent, data-driven action.

publications.title: UNDRR Annual Report 2025
publications.description: The UNDRR Annual Report 2025 presents several important achievements that UNDRR marked in the year 2025

publications.title: When digital systems fail: An expert report on the hidden risks of our digital world
publications.description: This expert report examines how digital infrastructure, now central to essential services such as healthcare, finance, and emergency response, is creating new forms of systemic risk through increasing interdependence.

publications.title: UNDRR Regional Office for Africa Quarterly Newsletter (Jan-Mar 2026)
publications.description: UNDRR's Regional Office for Africa has published its first quarterly newsletter of 2026, detailing the major achievements of the work being done by the organisation.

publications.title: Teaching resilience with the Stop Disasters game: a teacher's guide
publications.description: This Teacher’s Pack supports the use of the  Stop Disasters online game as a practical way to introduce disaster risk reduction (DRR) and resilience in the classroom.

publications.title: Concept note: The International day in memory of the victims of earthquakes
publications.description: The international day provides an annual opportunity for remembrance and renewed commitment to protecting lives and livelihoods from earthquake disasters.

publications.title: Study on the reach and effectiveness of Early Warning Systems in Timor-Leste
publications.description: This study evaluates the effectiveness of early warning systems in Timor-Leste, based on household surveys and focus group discussions across eight municipalities and key informant interviews with national, municipal and community stakeholders.

publications.title: Strengthening the disaster resilience of MSMEs in Barbados
publications.description: Strengthening the Disaster Resilience of MSMEs in Barbados

publications.title: Strengthening disaster resilience of SMEs through business continuity planning – Guidance and Templates
publications.description: This resource supports SMEs, including microenterprises, in developing a basic business continuity plan, with the aim of progressively aligning with leading international standards, particularly ISO 22301:2019 on business continuity management systems

publications.title: Sendai Framework Monitoring in Europe and Central Asia: A regional snapshot
publications.description: This report offers a snapshot of the aggregated data reported by Member States across the Europe and Central Asia region. It is based on data reported in the SFM system by 20 July 2025, following a previous edition of this report released in December 2020

publications.title: Flames of change II special report: Children and youth at the forefront of tackling urban heat
publications.description: This special report focuses on the important role that children and youth can play in tackling urban heat and the urban heat island effect. Both top-down and bottom-up approaches are highlighted to showcase comprehensive strategies in action.
